title: lupinus bicolor subsp. umbellatus
intro: Lupinus bicolor subsp. umbellatus, also known as the bush lupine, is a flowering plant native to the western coastal regions of North America. It is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Lupinus bicolor subsp. umbellatus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, although more research is needed to fully understand its effectiveness.
